#mistakenlyhuman

Humans are natural addicts. We want to keep doing something that we enjoy irrespective of either a negative or a positive reason. The easiest examples to witness are smoking and alcohol. But, we forget that we are creatures of habits. We have the tendency to give so much love, but when we don’t feel it reciprocated; we feel pain. Pain can be addictive. If nothing but pain is felt and seen for a long time, then it will consume the very being of you. We should not let negative things burden us for a lengthy stage. Because…

Once you have felt only pain for a very long time, you become addicted to it.

Follow @mistakenlyhuman for more such quotes

Everything gets better with time. Good things don't come easy in life.

Keep showing up, show your work to the world. If you are out there every single day and shout out what you have to say, eventually, they will hear you. Focus on the journey, enjoy it, it will lead you to the destination in the end. 

When we attach ourselves to the outcome, we sacrifice the process.

Be different. Challenge everything that deserves challenging.

The most under appreciated words in the world are “I love you”.

You make efforts for people you really care about.

Swipe to listen to the poem. Yes, this is about a girl. This is one of the poems that I wrote when I was falling in love with someone but the girl wasn’t sure of her feelings. This is me telling her what would I do if I fall in love with her and then asking her what would she do if she falls in love with me?

I own my poem and my voice!

There is beauty in everything we see. Sometimes, we just have to look hard enough. And when you find the beauty, make it precious. As someone said, “Beauty lies in the eyes of the observer.“

Appreciate Beauty. Even if you have to look far, wide or deep for it.

If someone says that they are a wise man, put on your shoes and run away in the opposite direction. A wise man will never say that he is wise. He will say he is just an idiot, but he has some thoughts and ideas that he wants to share with the world. Because after all …

We all are idiots, we just have to ensure that we are loveable idiots.

Any regret is like a slow poison. But a regret where there is no way to rectify is like an itch we can’t scratch. It happens when we have done something wrong to someone, but we can’t rectify it because the person is no more with us.

The worst regret in the world is one which you can do nothing about because the person it is related to is dead.

Human beings are social animals. We need companionship to feel complete. But this age of social media has made people addicted to their phones. The phone is not a companion, another human is. This leads to tinges of loneliness, anxiety and depression when we don’t get enough dopamine from social media. We crave for human intimacy at that point of time. It is safe to say that…

The value of companionship isn’t realised until it goes away.

The most underrated commodity in today’s world is hope. Humans are inherently hopeful beings. But today’s world is doing a marvellous job of crushing people’s hopes. Whenever we venture onto something new, we hope for it to succeed. That hope is the driving factor for us to put in the work. If we are not hopeful for anything to succeed, then we won’t try it. Let no one else extinguish that fire of hope within you. Protect your hope like you protect a child. Because…

Hope is what you are born with and leave behind when you die.

The only thing constant in life is change. If we avoid it and decide to live in the comfort zone only, then we will get left behind. Nothing good ever comes out of being in the comfort zone. In order to grow, the changes need embracing. Nobody successful will ever say that they achieved those heights by being in the comfort zone. We need to take risks to achieve our most ambitious goals. Hence…

Sometimes you gotta do what you don’t usually do.

We struggle with feelings for other human beings often. This doubt leads to overthinking. We can overthink about certain things in life, but here it doesn’t serve us well. We get more confused only. This uncertainty also challenges the trust we have in the other person. We can only know the genuine emotions for that person when we allow ourself to be in a situation where they aren’t a part of our life anymore. Hence…

The single easiest way to find out how you feel about someone, say goodbye.

Most people use sarcasm with the intention of humor or playfulness. But the inability or misunderstanding arises because of insecurities. Those insecurities make us receive sarcasm as hostile and filled with judgement. It can feel hurtful or hostile to the person on the receiving end of it. It frequently diminishes a feeling of trust and safety, but we can avoid all this, if we learn how to handle it.

Sarcasm is fun, it’s a pity few people understand it and then can handle it.

The thing about truths is that they will sting most of the times. But not allowing those truths to enter our life will be a life in denial. Truths teach us a lot more than any lie can. We must allow the truths to enter our lives no matter how much they hurt because we will not be living in anticipation of other things to surface. There is comfort in living our own lies to be secure in the bubble world we create. Eventually that bubble will burst and we will have to face reality, which is the ultimate truth of life.

Most of the truths are sad.

Patience is a virtue. Patience is the most important key in any journey. When travelling, it requires patience until we reach the destination. During cooking, we need patience until the vegetables soften. While on the journey of an author, it demands patience before reaching the audience. Success stories are told like they happened overnight, but we leave the patience of the person who achieved the success behind. 

If you learn to be patient on a small scale, then you and life will go a long way.

These days everyone has become subject to an external locus. They don’t have an identity of themselves. This is ironic that we are reading this on Social Media. But this is where people’s minds live these days. In order to get the message loud and clear, the messages need circulating here. All of us are talking about what others are doing. We no longer try to delve inside our own heads and have ideas and opinions of our own. In this society of abundant content, nobody wants to look into their lives and keep consuming other’s lives. Therefore…

People don’t want to spend their time thinking about the whys of their lives.

Overthinking is a frowned upon notion. It can be hinderances, obstructions in a person’s life to even do the mundane and routine tasks. But no one talks about the positive side of it. Nobody ever says that overthinking can be an effective and positive way to plan, to foresee, to be ready for the worst-case scenarios that life might throw at you. We associate overthinking with an evil reputation, but it has the capacity for becoming a gift…

Overthinking is a gift if only used wisely.

Humans are natural addicts. We want to keep doing something that we enjoy irrespective of either a negative or a positive reason. The easiest examples to witness are smoking and alcohol. But, we forget that we are creatures of habits. We have the tendency to give so much love, but when we don’t feel it reciprocated; we feel pain. Pain can be addictive. If nothing but pain is felt and seen for a long time, then it will consume the very being of you. We should not let negative things burden us for a lengthy stage. Because…

Once you have felt only pain for a very long time, you become addicted to it.

We have all heard, empty mind is home to the Devil. 

When we indulge ourselves in any activity, we don’t allow our mind to go onboard the overthinking train. 

When we have nothing to do, we allow our minds to overthink.

This leads our mind to perform the magic of permutations on all scenarios of our lives. 

It makes us to just ponder and do nothing.

The biggest culprit is free time and then the hyper-drive of overthinking becoming its fuel.

Most people use sarcasm with the intention of humor or playfulness. But the inability or misunderstanding arises because of insecurities. Those insecurities make us receive sarcasm as hostile and filled with judgement. It can feel hurtful or hostile to the person on the receiving end of it. It frequently diminishes a feeling of trust and safety, but we can avoid all this, if we learn how to handle it.

Sarcasm is fun, it’s a pity few people understand it and then can handle it.
